finding: After submitting the application the only confirmation is a small line of green text beside the button; the form does not clear, disable, or redirect, so a non-technical user cannot tell the submission succeeded and will resubmit.

evidence.quote: Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.

persona_rationale: My eyes were on the big orange SUBMIT button, not on a thin green line off to the right, and all my typed answers were still sitting in the boxes. To me that means it failed, so I would press submit again and again, then phone my grandson in a panic.

suggested_fix.current: Small inline green text 'Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.' next to the SUBMIT button; form stays filled, no redirect
suggested_fix.proposed: On success, redirect to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page (or show a full-width banner above the form), and clear/disable the form

finding: The three headline statistics render as '0+', '$0M+', '0+' instead of real figures, making the site look broken and untrustworthy.
persona_rationale: A zero means nobody has done this. Seeing '0 entrepreneurs launched' on the very first page made me doubt the whole company before I read a single word, and I nearly closed the tab.

suggested_fix.current: Counters show '0+', '$0M+', '0+'
suggested_fix.proposed: Render the real counts (e.g. '500+', '$12M+', '1,200+'); if a count is unknown at load, hide the stat rather than show 0

finding: There is no Contact link in the top navigation; the only way to reach /contact is the small footer link or knowing the URL.

evidence.quote:
ABOUT
RESOURCES
CASE STUDIES
NEWS
STEP INSIDE

persona_rationale: I scan the menu along the top for the word I want. 'Contact' isn't there, so I'd conclude there's no way to reach a real person — I would never think to scroll all the way to the bottom and hunt in the small grey writing.

suggested_fix.current: Top nav: About, Resources, Case Studies, News (no Contact)
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Contact' link to the top navigation

finding: The navigation item and route say 'NEWS' but the page heading is 'BLOG', and article breadcrumbs also say 'BLOG' — the same section has two different names.

evidence.quote: BLOG

persona_rationale: I clicked the word NEWS and landed on a page shouting BLOG. I immediately worry I clicked the wrong thing or ended up somewhere I shouldn't be, because the words don't match.

suggested_fix.current: Nav says 'NEWS', page heading and breadcrumb say 'BLOG'
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one name everywhere — change the page heading and breadcrumb to 'News' to match the nav item and the /news route

finding: On first load the article cards render as blank outlines until the user scrolls (scroll-reveal animation), so the page looks broken or half-loaded above the fold.

evidence.quote: they render as BLANK card outlines until scrolled into view

persona_rationale: Empty boxes where I expect articles tell me the page didn't finish loading. I'd wait, refresh, or leave — I wouldn't know that scrolling makes the content appear.

suggested_fix.current: Article cards are invisible until scrolled into view
suggested_fix.proposed: Render the first row of article cards visible on load; only apply reveal animation to cards genuinely below the fold

finding: On first open of the page editor a 'Quick Tour' overlay sits on top of the outline panel it points at and blocks interaction until dismissed.

evidence.quote: a "Quick Tour" overlay (Step 1 of 3) covers the outline panel and blocks interaction until dismissed

persona_rationale: A pop-up that covers the very button it's telling me to press is the kind of thing that makes me certain I've broken the computer. I'd freeze and not touch anything.

suggested_fix.current: Quick Tour overlay obscures the panel/button it references
suggested_fix.proposed: Position the tour tooltip beside (not over) its target, and let the user dismiss or skip the whole tour with one obvious button

finding: The admin sidebar has no News/Blog section; /admin/news is only reachable by typing the URL.

evidence.quote: NO News/Blog item in the sidebar

persona_rationale: If my grandson asked me to add a news post, I'd look in the menu, not find it, and give up entirely — I have no idea how to 'type a URL' to reach a hidden page.

suggested_fix.current: Sidebar has only SEO pages, Media library, Settings
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'News' (or 'Blog') item to the admin sidebar linking to /admin/news

finding: The homepage produces a horizontal scrollbar at mobile width.

evidence.quote: mobile-375 | home-003-mobile.png | horizontal scroll present

persona_rationale: A sideways scrollbar on my iPad always makes me think I've zoomed or stretched something by accident, and I waste time trying to 'fix' it.

suggested_fix.current: Page content exceeds viewport width at 375px causing horizontal scroll
suggested_fix.proposed: Constrain full-bleed sections to 100vw / add overflow-x guards so the page fits the screen width

finding: An article thumbnail image fails to load with a 500 error on the news listing page.

evidence.quote: 500 /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F...png&w=640&q=75

persona_rationale: A broken picture tells me nobody is looking after this website, and that makes me nervous to type my name and email into it.

suggested_fix.current: Image returns 500 (broken external CDN URL via /_next/image)
suggested_fix.proposed: Re-host the image locally / fix the source URL, and show a placeholder image if a thumbnail fails rather than a broken box

finding: Page status and SEO-readiness indicators are 10x10px buttons and row actions hide behind small three-dot summaries.

evidence.quote: small touch target: button "SEO readiness: Blocked" 10x10px
small touch target: button "Page status: Draft" 10x10px

persona_rationale: My fingers aren't steady and my eyes aren't sharp. A 10-pixel dot is impossible for me to see the meaning of or to tap without hitting the wrong thing.

suggested_fix.current: 10x10px status dots and icon-only '⋮' action buttons
suggested_fix.proposed: Enlarge interactive status/action targets to at least 44x44px and pair status dots with a visible text label

finding: The primary CTA in the header is labelled 'STEP INSIDE', which does not clearly say it opens the application form.

evidence.quote: STEP INSIDE

persona_rationale: 'Step inside' sounds like a doorway in a shop, not a button. I wouldn't know it takes me to the application, so I'd hunt for something clearer.

suggested_fix.current: Header button: 'STEP INSIDE' -> /apply
suggested_fix.proposed: Label it 'Apply Now' to match its destination and the page CTAs

finding: The top navigation has no 'Home' link; returning home depends on knowing the logo is clickable.

evidence.quote:
ABOUT
RESOURCES
CASE STUDIES
NEWS
STEP INSIDE

persona_rationale: I don't know that pictures (the logo) are buttons. If I want to get back to the start I look for the word 'Home' in the menu, and it isn't there.

suggested_fix.current: No 'Home' item in the top nav (logo links home but isn't an obvious link)
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Home' item to the top nav, or add visible hover styling so the logo reads as clickable

finding: The 'Resources' nav item links to /pre-call-resources, a page aimed at people who have already booked a call, not general resources.

evidence.quote:
link "Resources":
      - /url: /pre-call-resources
PREPARE FOR YOUR VENDING STRATEGY CALL.

persona_rationale: I clicked 'Resources' expecting helpful reading, and got a page about preparing for a phone call I haven't booked. That mismatch makes me feel I clicked the wrong button.

suggested_fix.current: 'Resources' nav -> /pre-call-resources (call-prep content)
suggested_fix.proposed: Rename the nav item to 'Pre-Call Prep', or point 'Resources' at a general resources page

finding: Submitting the empty form surfaces only the browser's native single-field tooltip 'Please fill out this field.' with no on-page list of what is missing.

evidence.quote: empty: no visible validation errors after empty submit

persona_rationale: The little 'Please fill out this field' bubble did help me, but it only shows one field at a time and vanishes — I'd like the page itself to tell me, in plain words, every box I still need to fill.

suggested_fix.current: Relies solely on the browser's native validation tooltip, one field at a time
suggested_fix.proposed: Show inline messages under each required field on submit (e.g. 'Please enter your name') so all missing fields are visible at once

finding: Submitting the forgot-password form with empty or invalid input shows no visible validation feedback.

evidence.quote: empty: no visible validation errors after empty submit

persona_rationale: Resetting a password is exactly when I'm already anxious. If I press the button and nothing visibly happens, I won't know whether to wait, retry, or that I typed my email wrong.

suggested_fix.current: No visible error or confirmation after submitting the reset form
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a clear confirmation ('If that email exists, we've sent a reset link') and an inline error for an empty/invalid email

finding: The contact form's success/failure feedback could not be observed (submit was skipped for safety), and the apply form's known-weak inline-only confirmation suggests contact likely shares the same pattern.

evidence.quote: external-bucket form: filled for visual review, NOT submitted

persona_rationale: When I send a message to a real person I need the screen to plainly say 'Your message was sent.' If contact mirrors the apply form's tiny inline note, I'd never be sure my question got through.

suggested_fix.current: Contact submit feedback unverified; apply form uses a small inline note
suggested_fix.proposed: Ensure the contact form shows a prominent success confirmation (banner or dedicated thank-you state) on submit

finding: There is no discoverable archive/delete path for a page from the editor or the list row menu, so an admin cannot retire a page they created.

evidence.quote: the builder editor offers no archive/delete path that automation could find

persona_rationale: If I made a page by mistake, I'd desperately want an obvious 'undo' or 'delete'. Not being able to find one would leave me stuck with my error and afraid I'd made a permanent mess.

suggested_fix.current: No visible Archive/Delete in the page editor or list row actions
suggested_fix.proposed: Add an explicit 'Archive page' control in the editor and an 'Archive' item in the list row '⋮' menu, with a confirm dialog

finding: The Redirects manager is an orphaned route with no inbound link from any explored admin page.

evidence.quote: Redirects | NO PATH FOUND

persona_rationale: A page with no way to reach it from any menu might as well not exist for someone like me. I only ever use what I can see and click.

suggested_fix.current: /admin/pages/redirects has no inbound navigation
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Redirects' link under the SEO pages section of the admin sidebar

finding: The same content type is named four different ways across the product: 'News' (route/public nav), 'Blog CMS'/'New blog post' (editor), 'BLOG' (public heading).

evidence.quote: "News" in the route, "Blog CMS"/"New blog post" in the editor, "NEWS" in public nav, "BLOG" as the public page heading

persona_rationale: Four names for one thing means I can never be sure I'm in the right place. Consistency is the only way I learn an app.

suggested_fix.current: Mixed 'News'/'Blog'/'Blog CMS' naming for one content type
suggested_fix.proposed: Pick one term ('News') and use it for the route, nav, editor breadcrumb, editor heading, and public page heading

finding: News list rows offer no archive/delete affordance; retiring a post requires opening each post's editor to find the Archive button.

evidence.quote: List rows offer no destructive/archive affordance; archiving requires opening each post's editor

persona_rationale: If I want to tidy up a mistaken post, I'd expect a clear option right there in the list. Having to dig inside each one makes me afraid I'll change the wrong thing along the way.

suggested_fix.current: No archive/delete in the news list row
suggested_fix.proposed: Add an 'Archive' action to each news list row with a confirmation prompt

finding: The homepage records a high cumulative layout shift (CLS 0.263), meaning content jumps around as it loads.

evidence.quote: Layout shift (CLS): 0.263

persona_rationale: When things jump on the screen while I'm reading, I lose my place and sometimes tap a button I didn't mean to — which is the thing I fear most.

suggested_fix.current: CLS 0.263 on the homepage
suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for images/fonts (set width/height, use font-display swap with metrics) to bring CLS under 0.1
